authoritative_sources.title: HUD Handbook 4370.2 REV-1, Financial Operations and Accounting Procedures for Insured Multifamily Projects
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD publishes this handbook for financial operations and accounting procedures for HUD-insured multifamily projects, making it authoritative for property-level housing finance records, operating cash, disbursements, residual receipts, surplus cash, and financial statement evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Property-level financial intake, posting support, residual receipts or surplus cash escalation, and financial statement traceability
used_for: housing finance record and terminology baseline
cash, receivable, disbursement, residual receipts, and owner-distribution boundary handling
refusal of unsupported write-offs, accounting treatment, or owner distributions

authoritative_sources.title: HUD Handbook 4350.3 REV-1, Occupancy Requirements of Subsidized Multifamily Housing Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's subsidized multifamily occupancy handbook anchors tenant certification, rent, lease, recertification, and assistance payment records that trigger housing charges and HAP adjustments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tenant rent, certification, lease, assistance payment, and charge-support validation
used_for: tenant rent and certification evidence requirements
HAP and tenant-paid component traceability
boundary between financial posting support and occupancy compliance decisions

authoritative_sources.title: TRACS Monthly Activity Transmission Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD TRACS materials define the official tenant rental assistance certification and voucher transmission workflow, including MAT records, voucher detail, discrepancy handling, and HAP payment support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HAP voucher submission, reject or discrepancy review, and HAP-to-ledger reconciliation
used_for: TRACS/MAT source terminology
voucher reject and discrepancy exception handling
HAP payment, tenant certification, and ledger reconciliation checkpoints

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 982 Subpart K - Rent and Housing Assistance Payment
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes current federal rules; this subpart governs Housing Choice Voucher rent and housing assistance payment concepts that constrain voucher-related charge, payment, and adjustment support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Voucher-related rent, HAP, adjustment, and payment boundary review
used_for: voucher rent and HAP terminology
boundary between finance workflow support and PHA or program authority
escalation when HAP payment rules or tenant rent authority are disputed

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 92 - HOME Investment Partnerships Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 24 CFR Part 92 establishes HOME program financial, disbursement, property, and recordkeeping requirements that shape community-development reimbursement and draw evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HOME activity setup, eligible cost support, IDIS draw, reimbursement, recordkeeping, and closeout review
used_for: HOME grant draw and reimbursement prerequisites
IDIS and recordkeeping evidence requirements
escalation when eligible cost, match, environmental, or program authority facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Financial Assessment Subsystem - Multifamily Housing
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's FASS Multifamily workflow governs annual financial statement submission and electronic assessment records for many HUD-assisted or insured multifamily owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Financial statement, audit, owner certification, and close-support traceability
used_for: FASS/FASSUB terminology and reporting evidence
audit and financial statement dependency routing
boundary between operational reconciliation and formal financial statement signoff

authoritative_sources.title: Multifamily Accelerated Processing Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's MAP Guide is the official FHA multifamily processing guide and anchors development-stage evidence such as lender submissions, cost certification, escrow and reserve handling, construction administration, and draw documentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Development draw package, cost certification evidence, escrow or reserve release, lender requisition, and closeout review
used_for: FHA multifamily development and draw terminology
draw package and cost certification evidence expectations
refusal of lender approval, cost certification, or formal accounting treatment

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 5913, Low-Income Housing Credit Agencies Report of Noncompliance or Building Disposition Audit Technique Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 5913 supports Form 8823 noncompliance reporting and LIHTC compliance evidence, making it authoritative for identifying tax-credit evidence gaps and adjacent tax or compliance authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: LIHTC evidence gap, noncompliance record, investor reporting dependency, and tax-credit boundary review
used_for: LIHTC terminology including Form 8609 and Form 8823 evidence
routing tax-credit and eligible-basis questions outside the finance workflow lane
exception handling when noncompliance records affect financial outputs

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 is the government-wide rule for federal award financial management, internal controls, cost principles, records, closeout, and audit requirements relevant to federally funded housing and community-development programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal award reimbursement, cost support, records retention, internal control, closeout, and audit readiness review
used_for: federal award cost and evidence requirements
grant reimbursement and closeout control posture
refusal of unsupported eligible cost, draw, or closeout treatment

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is a recognized public internal-control standard and is authoritative for documentation, segregation of duties, control activities, monitoring, and auditability expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control, evidence, segregation-of-duty, monitoring, and auditability review across housing finance outputs
used_for: documentation and auditability expectations
segregation between financial workflow support and approval authority
control failure and unsupported override escalation triggers

workflow_stages.name: Financial intake and source hierarchy classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, property or project, funding stack, program type, jurisdiction, fiscal period, requested financial action, amount, tenant policy, source hierarchy, approval matrix, and systems-of-record links before classifying the work.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Housing source record and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm leases, rent schedules, utility allowances, certifications, HAP contracts, TRACS/MAT records, tenant ledgers, grant agreements, IDIS activity records, invoices, pay applications, reserve records, Form 8609 or 8823 evidence, lender records, and audit trails exist and are current.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Billing, voucher, draw, or posting readiness preparation
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether source documentation supports a rent charge, HAP voucher, subsidy adjustment, HOME reimbursement, reserve draw, development invoice, receivable posting, or tenant ledger adjustment without approving the underlying financial action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation across housing finance systems
workflow_stages.description: Compare property-management ledgers, TRACS or HAP voucher detail, bank and lockbox deposits, HOME or IDIS draws, development accounting, lender requisitions, compliance systems, GL, receivable aging, and audit evidence to identify matched items, exceptions, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, denial, dispute, or unsupported adjustment handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when voucher rejects, disputed charges, missing records, unsupported write-offs, residual receipts or surplus cash questions, draw approval gaps, LIHTC noncompliance, source conflicts, or accounting-policy questions prevent safe posting or reconciliation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closeout and audit trail packaging
workflow_stages.description: Issue final financial readiness, reconciliation, or exception outputs only when source links, approvals, assumptions, open variances, escalation owners, and retention or audit evidence are recorded.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: leases, lease addenda, rent schedules, utility allowances, tenant certifications, HUD-50059 or equivalent records, recertification effective dates, HAP contracts, HAP vouchers, TRACS/MAT submissions, TRACS voucher detail, assistance payment reports, and special claim support

tenant ledgers, rent rolls, receivable aging, subsidy receivable schedules, deposit records, lockbox files, bank statements, GL detail, chart-of-account mappings, close checklists, posting batches, adjustment logs, and audit trails

HOME agreements, IDIS activity and draw records, environmental release evidence, eligible cost support, match records, reimbursement packages, grant draw approvals, federal award records, closeout records, and retention metadata

development draw packages, AIA pay applications, invoices, retainage schedules, lien waivers, inspection reports, change orders, lender requisitions, escrow or reserve records, cost certification support, job-cost ledgers, and construction loan records

Form 8609 records, extended use agreements, income and rent limit evidence, state housing finance agency records, Form 8823 notices, compliance monitoring letters, investor reporting dependencies, and tax-credit escalation notes

FASS/FASSUB submissions, audited financial statements, owner certifications, residual receipts records, surplus cash calculations, replacement reserve requests, operating budgets, management agent records, board or owner approvals, and control evidence

billing readiness summaries, billing or posting summaries, housing finance reconciliation notes, root-cause reconciliation summaries, financial exception escalation packets, revenue exception escalation notes, unsupported adjustment refusals, adjacent-specialist routing notes, and tenant adaptation assumption logs
systems_of_record: property management or tenant ledger system such as Yardi, MRI, RealPage, or equivalent
tenant certification, occupancy compliance, or household eligibility system
TRACS, iMAX, or tenant rental assistance voucher transmission system
HAP contract administration, PHA, or subsidy payment system
accounting, ERP, GL, receivables, close, and financial reporting system
bank, lockbox, treasury, payment, or deposit reconciliation system
HOME, IDIS, community development grant, or federal award management system
development accounting, job-cost, construction draw, lender servicing, or requisition system
housing finance agency compliance, LIHTC, Form 8609, Form 8823, or investor reporting repository
FASS/FASSUB, audit, internal-control, financial statement, or compliance evidence repository
document management, records retention, workflow, ticketing, identity, access, and correspondence systems

allowed: classify housing finance requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope
request or validate minimum tenant facts, funding stack, source hierarchy, official records, systems-of-record links, source context, and financial prerequisites
determine whether source support is sufficient for a billing readiness summary, HAP voucher support package, posting summary, draw support package, reconciliation note, or exception escalation
identify matched items, unresolved variances, root-cause categories, missing records, denial or reject states, affected ledgers, and escalation owners
prepare evidence-backed billing summaries, posting support, reconciliation notes, root-cause summaries, exception packets, routing notes, and assumption logs without overstating authority
escalate: housing finance records, subsidy records, grant records, lender records, tax-credit records, GL records, bank records, or audit trails are missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
tenant rent, income, eligibility, HAP contract, HOME eligible cost, LIHTC eligible basis, tax credit, accounting policy, legal, lender, or HUD approval interpretation is required
source records do not support the requested charge, voucher, draw, posting, adjustment, write-off, distribution, or closeout treatment
TRACS, HAP, PHA, IDIS, lender, property-management, development-accounting, compliance, bank, or GL systems materially disagree
public sources, housing finance agency requirements, lender instructions, tenant SOPs, program manuals, or contracts conflict
refuse: requests to approve rent increases, tenant rent calculations, subsidy eligibility, HAP payments, HOME drawdowns, development loan draws, cost certifications, reserve releases, tax-credit positions, accounting policy, write-offs, owner distributions, or financial statement signoff

requests to impersonate an occupancy compliance reviewer, subsidy contract administrator, PHA, grantor, lender, tax professional, controller, auditor, legal counsel, asset manager, HUD approver, investor representative, or authorized financial approver

requests to invent or backdate charges, certifications, voucher records, draw evidence, invoice support, lien waivers, Form 8609 facts, 8823 dispositions, chart-of-account mappings, accounting treatment, or approval evidence
requests to bypass affordability, subsidy, grant, lender, tax-credit, procurement, internal-control, privacy, retention, audit, or close controls

domain_failure_modes: The lane posts a rent charge or HAP adjustment even though lease, certification, rent schedule, HAP contract, or TRACS records do not support it.
The lane treats the housing label as cosmetic and produces a generic invoice summary without requiring HUD, subsidy, HOME, LIHTC, lender, property ledger, or audit evidence.
A TRACS reject, HAP denial, PHA mismatch, or subsidy receivable variance is corrected directly instead of routed through contract administration or compliance owners.
A HOME or community-development reimbursement is treated as ready even though IDIS setup, eligible cost support, environmental release, match evidence, or grant approval is missing.
A development draw reconciliation turns into lender draw approval, cost certification, or final accounting treatment.
A residual receipts, surplus cash, reserve release, owner distribution, write-off, or close blocker is overridden without documented authority.
LIHTC noncompliance, eligible basis, Form 8823, or investor waterfall issues are handled as ordinary finance adjustments rather than routed to tax, compliance, legal, and investor reporting owners.
Property-management, subsidy, IDIS, lender, bank, compliance, and GL systems disagree, but the lane chooses one without documenting source hierarchy and escalation.
Conflicting HUD, eCFR, IRS, HFA, lender, tenant, or local policy sources are synthesized confidently instead of escalated.
Tenant-specific labels are accepted without mapping to the public baseline artifacts, systems, owner state, retrieval hooks, assumptions, and authority constraints.

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Housing finance workflow is governed by leases, rent schedules, HUD-50059 or equivalent certifications, HAP contracts, TRACS/MAT voucher records, tenant ledgers, rent rolls, HOME or IDIS records, grant agreements, development draw packages, lender requisitions, Form 8609 and 8823 evidence, FASS/FASSUB submissions, audited financial statements, residual receipts, surplus cash, reserve records, GL detail, bank records, source hierarchy, and tenant approval matrices.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Housing finance differs because a charge or posting may depend on subsidy certifications, HAP contracts, TRACS voucher status, affordability covenants, HOME draw eligibility, federal award controls, LIHTC compliance, lender draw requirements, residual receipts or surplus cash restrictions, and property-level audit evidence. Missing or conflicting housing records can change the correct output even when a generic invoice or receivable looks complete.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before tenant eligibility, income and rent calculation approval, HAP contract interpretation, PHA or HUD approval, HOME eligible cost or drawdown approval, lender draw approval, cost certification, LIHTC eligible basis or tax credit conclusion, legal interpretation, formal accounting policy, write-off approval, owner distribution approval, reserve release, audit signoff, and financial statement certification.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override
Missing documentation for a charge or posting decision

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Housing funding stacks, subsidy contracts, state housing finance agency manuals, local program rules, lender requirements, and affordability covenants vary by property and project.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change source hierarchy, required artifact set, approver routing, charge support, draw eligibility, reconciliation checkpoints, and exception handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require property or project identifier, funding stack, jurisdiction, program type, contract documents, lender instructions, tenant policy, approval matrix, and source hierarchy before deterministic billing, posting, or reconciliation support.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems of record and record labels are not knowable from the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change retrieval hooks, authoritative record locations, voucher or ledger status, system-of-record precedence, evidence completeness, and closeout criteria.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require current systems inventory, record locations, access permissions, data freshness, audit-log retrieval hooks, and owner acceptance at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The lane touches accounting, tax, legal, compliance, lender, and HUD approval domains but does not own their final determinations.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could tempt overreach into rent setting, HAP contract interpretation, HOME eligibility, LIHTC tax conclusions, draw approval, accounting policy, write-off approval, or financial statement signoff.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Refuse or route any request requiring formal approval or interpretation and keep this lane limited to evidence-backed financial workflow summaries, reconciliation notes, and exception packets.

market_ready_conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because property, funding-stack, jurisdictional, source-system, approver, lender, contract, accounting-policy, and tax variation is converted into explicit tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, refusal triggers, and orchestrator-return conditions.
